title: Thlaspi cochlearinum
taxonomy: Thlaspi cochlearinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Thlaspi and is classified under the kingdom Plantae. Its classification is further divided into the phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Brassicales, and family Brassicaceae. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, it shares similarities with other plants such as cabbage and mustard. Thlaspi cochlearinum's classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.

It is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin infections. intro: Thlaspi cochlearinum, also known as corn spurry, is a low-growing, annual plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi cochlearinum include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thlaspi cochlearinum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
